2604.05876.title: Mechanistic Circuit-Based Knowledge Editing in Large Language Models
2604.05876.paper_summary: Deploying Large Language Models (LLMs) in real-world dynamic environments raises the challenge of updating their pre-trained knowledge. While existing knowledge editing methods can reliably patch isolated facts, they frequently suffer from a "Reasoning Gap", where the model recalls the edited fact but fails to utilize it in multi-step reasoning chains. To bridge this gap, we introduce MCircKE (\underline{M}echanistic \underline{Circ}uit-based \underline{K}nowledge \underline{E}diting), a novel framework that enables a precise "map-and-adapt" editing procedure. MCircKE first identifies the causal circuits responsible for a specific reasoning task, capturing both the storage of the fact and the routing of its logical consequences. It then surgically update parameters exclusively within this mapped circuit. Extensive experiments on the MQuAKE-3K benchmark demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method for multi-hop reasoning in knowledge editing.
2604.05876.paper_summary_zh: 在現實世界的動態環境中部署大型語言模型（LLMs）帶來了更新其預訓練知識的挑戰。雖然現有的知識編輯方法可以可靠地修補孤立的事實，但它們經常遭遇“推理差距”，即模型能夠回憶起編輯過的事實，但在多步推理鏈中無法利用它。為了彌補這一差距，我們引入了 MCircKE（\underline{M}echanistic \underline{Circ}uit-based \underline{K}nowledge \underline{E}diting），這是一個新穎的框架，能夠實現精確的“映射與適應”編輯程序。MCircKE 首先識別出負責特定推理任務的因果電路，捕捉事實的存儲以及其邏輯後果的路由。然後，它僅在這個映射的電路內外科性地更新參數。在 MQuAKE-3K 基準上的大量實驗證明了所提出的方法在知識編輯中的多跳推理的有效性。
2604.05876.author: Tianyi Zhao et.al.
2604.05876.authors: Tianyi Zhao, Yinhan He, Wendy Zheng, Chen Chen
